headline: The Iberian lynx is back from the brink of extinction
meta_description: The Iberian lynx has recovered from under 100 animals to thousands, moving off the endangered list — proof conservation works.
summary_short: Once down to fewer than a hundred animals, the world's rarest wild cat has multiplied more than twentyfold and is no longer endangered.
body:
At the turn of the century, fewer than a hundred Iberian lynxes survived in the wild, making it the most endangered cat on Earth. Two decades of stubborn work — breeding centres, rabbit recovery, wildlife corridors across Spain and Portugal — changed the story completely.

Today thousands of lynxes roam the Iberian peninsula, and the IUCN has moved the species from Endangered to Vulnerable: one of the greatest recoveries of any cat species ever recorded. The spotted ghost of the Mediterranean scrub is a ghost no more — proof that when people decide a species is worth saving, it can be saved.
